variables.content: The quasiparticle effective mass ratio m*/m, where m* denotes the effective quasiparticle mass inferred from the linear (normal‑state) specific heat and m denotes the bare 3He atomic mass, determined from the measured normal‑state specific heat using the calorimetry procedure described above, is in close agreement with the values recently reported by Alvesalo et al. (the Helsinki group) and both the Helsinki results and the present measurements lie approximately thirty percent below the values reported by Abel et al., so that these experimental specific‑heat determinations support the lower effective‑mass values reported by the Helsinki group rather than the earlier, larger values of Abel et al.

Heat-capacity measurements of liquid 3He that were reported by Alvesalo et al. using the Helsinki temperature scale are not subject to large errors traceable to the Helsinki temperature calibration, because the Helsinki scale value T_c = 1.04 mK agrees with the NBS-determined T_c = 1.025 ± 0.02 mK to within 1.4% and within the quoted experimental uncertainties; a fractional temperature-scale error of order 1–2% is therefore too small to account for an approximately 30% discrepancy previously observed in the inferred effective mass m* from those heat-capacity data.
      T. A. Alvesalo, T. Haavasoja, M. T. Manninen, and A. T. Soinne, Phys. Rev. Lett. 44, 1076 (1980)

If the Helsinki platinum-NMR temperature scale that was used to assign temperatures in the earlier liquid $^3$He heat-capacity measurements of Alvesalo et al. were systematically in error by an amount sufficient to explain the previously reported deviation from earlier heat-capacity values, then an independent intercomparison that establishes agreement between the Helsinki platinum-NMR scale and the NBS-CTS-1 scale at the relevant millikelvin fixed points to within about 1% removes the platinum-NMR temperature calibration as the primary plausible source of the discrepancy in the Alvesalo et al. heat-capacity dataset.
      Alvesalo, T.A., Haavasoja, T., Manninen, M.T., and Soinne, A.T., Phys. Rev. Lett. 44 (1980) 1076.

For temperatures T < 100 mK the measured heat capacity of the sample is well described by C(T) = γ T with γ = 66 J K−2 per mole of 3He (where one mole corresponds to Avogadro's number NA of atoms and R = 8.314 J mol−1 K−1 is the gas constant), which gives C/(R T) = 8 K−1; interpreting γ as the Fermi-liquid linear specific-heat coefficient of liquid 3He contained within the droplets and applying the standard Fermi-liquid proportionality between γ and the quasiparticle effective mass m* (with m3 denoting the bare atomic mass of 3He), the measured γ corresponds to an effective mass ratio m*/m3 = 10.2 ± 0.5 under the assumption that the droplet molar volume and density of states prefactors are close to their bulk values.

The molar specific heat per mole of liquid {}^{3}\mathrm{He}, expressed as C/(nR T) where C is the measured heat capacity, n is the amount of substance in moles, and R is the molar gas constant, is linear in temperature for temperatures above approximately 3\ \mathrm{mK} and can be written C/(nR T)=\gamma with the experimentally determined average value \gamma=2.11\pm 0.02\ \mathrm{K}^{-1}, the average being taken over fifteen experimental runs performed at nominally zero pressure.
